instrumentationDataset:
# UX-flow instrumentation dataset — 2026-07-16 (staging @66fef654f, post-sweep)
Viewport 1440×900 (fold = 900px). Live DB, Expero workspace (data-rich, GSC+GA4+Webflow connected, no DataForSEO). "aboveFold" = visible in-page action controls above the first fold. "firstPrimary" = y-position of the first enabled primary-variant button.

| Surface (route) | Page height | Folds deep | Actions (in-page) | Above fold | First primary action |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Cockpit (home) | 1,203 | 1.3 | 18 | 16 | — (all secondary; verdict-first, healthy) |
| **Insights Engine (seo-strategy)** | **3,033** | **3.4** | **19** | **1** | **"Stage for issue" @1750 — two folds down** |
| Keywords (seo-keywords) | 3,498 | 3.9 | 22 | 19 | "Add" @87 (top-loaded; depth = table content) |
| Brand & AI (brand) | 2,033 | 2.3 | 20 | 9 | readiness cards @267 double as nav |
| **Search & Traffic (analytics-hub)** | **32,096** | **35.7** | 57 | 13 | — (uncapped 500-row GSC detail table in page flow) |
| Content Pipeline | 900 | 1.0 | 8 | 8 | board fits one fold |
| **Site Audit (seo-audit)** | **42,486** | **47.2** | 24 | 18 | "Accept all 785" @764 (toolbar good; unbounded issue list below) |
| SEO Editor | 993 | 1.1 | 12 | 12 | contained worksheet — the GOOD pattern |
| Competitors | 900 | 1.0 | 3 | 3 | setup state (provider unconfigured) |
| Links | 900 | 1.0 | 6 | 6 | empty-scan state, centered CTA |
| **Schema (seo-schema)** | **34,404** | **38.2** | **541** | 10 | "Generate all pages" @362 (766-URL table unbounded) |
| **Media (asset-manager)** | **24,702** | **27.4** | **1,583** | 43 | "Upload" @326 (asset grid unbounded) |
| Page Intelligence | 900 | 1.0 | 791 | 19 | master/detail with internal scroll — the GOOD pattern |
| Diagnostics (global) | 5,036 | 5.6 | 22 | 4 | @78 |

Earlier-walk qualitative notes (same session): Outcomes/Action Results leads with the Record-published-work form ABOVE its readback tabs (inversion, audit-confirmed); Global Settings opens on a ~30-chip monospace SC-DOMAIN wall; Roadmap is metrics→filters→list (healthy); Requests empty state is well-worded; Local Presence ~2 folds, honest states; Performance empty state honest; Page Rewriter two-pane contained.

## Headline patterns
1. **Engine is the unique action-inversion**: the only surface whose primary operator verbs (Stage/Details per rec) sit ≥2 folds down with only 1 in-page action above the fold; "Setup and operator tools" at the literal page bottom (y≈2940). Topbar holds meta-actions only (Update/Regenerate/Refresh/Send-0-staged).
2. **Four scroll canyons** — Site Audit (47.2 folds), Schema (38.2), Search & Traffic (35.7), Media (27.4) — all render unbounded collections into page flow. The platform already ships the correct containment pattern in SEO Editor (bounded worksheet) and Page Intelligence (master/detail internal scroll): the fix is applying an existing idiom, not inventing one.
3. Everything else is 1–2.3 folds and top-loaded: the shallow surfaces are genuinely healthy.
4. **⌘K palette is navigation-only**: admin destinations + workspace switcher; zero action verbs (no run-audit/send/regenerate/stage). Palette also lists debug workspaces (cascade-debug-*, dbg*, Trigger Check WS) alongside real clients.
5. Keywords' depth is content (table), not misplacement — its issue remains the four stacked filter layers (lens tray + filter chips + sort chips + More filters) and "Client keyword feedback" buried below the 50-row table (y≈3411).

## Data-coverage addendum (measured vs code-judged)
- **Measured on real production-shaped data (Expero, live DB):** Cockpit, Engine (99 recs), Keywords (5.9K rows), Search & Traffic (real 500-row GSC), Site Audit (766 URLs), Schema (766 URLs), Media (real asset library), SEO Editor (536 targets), Brand & AI, Page Intelligence, Diagnostics, Roadmap, Settings. All canyon/inversion findings rest on real load.
- **Links re-measured POPULATED** after running a real redirect scan on Expero (613 redirects, scanned 21:43): pageH stays 900 / 1 fold — **Links contains its collection correctly under load; not a canyon.**
- **Empty/unconfigured only (populated flow NOT live-measured):** Competitors (requires DataForSEO credentials — none exist locally; populated stack covered only by component-test fixtures), Performance (no page-weight scan artifact; lens code is bounded by design — Top-N bulk, paired cards), Pipeline board + Cockpit queue under load (no fixture stresses them; the seeded demo workspaces are light), Requests/Outcomes populated. These carry a "code-judged, not live-measured" caveat in any recommendation touching them.
- Fixture reality check: `Provider Rich Studio` / `Demo Rich CMS` seed provider-style rows but NO scan artifacts and no board load — a seed-fixture gap worth noting as its own recommendation (a "loaded demo workspace" would make future UX audits and flag-ON smokes much stronger).

judges.headline: The Engine's single spine is a well-told story read top-to-bottom, but the operator's daily job runs bottom-to-top: with 1 of 19 in-page actions above the fold, the first "Stage for issue" verb at y=1750, and operator tools at y≈2940, the parity contract's own circle-back trigger ("revisit if the single spine makes a required action materially harder to find") has measurably fired — and the cheapest fix is already half-built, since the ?lens= scroll/focus receiver exists with zero visible senders.

findings.finding: Action inversion is structural, not incidental: the operator's daily loop (review moves → stage → send) begins two folds down, below ~1600px of read-only narrative (eyebrow toolbar, StrategyDiff, staleness nudges, verdict hero, 4-tile value frame, POV card, stance bar, 4 signals + lost-query card).
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md line 7: Insights Engine 3,033px / 3.4 folds / 19 actions / 1 above fold / first primary 'Stage for issue' @1750. Section order in EngineSurface.tsx:593-960 — the BackingMovesQueue (the only home of Stage/Details/Fix/Park verbs per parity contract) mounts ninth of twelve blocks.

findings.finding: The parity contract's owner-recorded circle-back condition has fired, so a reorder is an owner decision that is already contractually anticipated — not a violation of the approved single-spine model (ODP-001 A).
findings.evidence: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/engine-contract.md:135 — 'Circle-back trigger: revisit if the single spine makes a required review/action materially harder to find.' The dataset provides the quantitative proof (headline pattern 1: 'Engine is the unique action-inversion'), and the owner's own words ('user actions at the top — currently the insights engine controls live at the very bottom') restate the trigger.

findings.finding: The in-page navigation machinery is fully built but has zero visible senders: ENGINE_LENSES defines six labeled anchors (Spine/Changes/Signals/POV/Moves/Operations), useEngineSurfaceState validates and persists ?lens=, and EngineSurface implements the scroll+focus receiver — yet no UI ever renders the labels, so the only way to jump is hand-typing a URL param.
findings.evidence: useEngineSurfaceState.ts:8-15 (labels rendered nowhere — repo grep shows setLens called only from the attention-strip handler at EngineSurface.tsx:458); EngineSurface.tsx:500-513 (working scrollIntoView+focus receiver); prior audit Q5: 'Six surfaces carry URL lens vocabulary larger than their visible UI.'

findings.finding: Operations at the literal page bottom (y≈2940) is an owner-approved additive seam (V5), but it is unreachable without a full 3.4-fold scroll: there is no in-page affordance pointing at it, only the unadvertised ?lens=operations deep link. The frequently-needed rows inside it (Recommendation history, Setup & handoffs, config panel) inherit the same burial.
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md headline 1 ('Setup and operator tools at the literal bottom, y≈2940'); engine-contract.md:159 ('keep Operations below the prototype spine ... intentional additive seam') and V5 approval at :220; EngineOperations.tsx:121-327 shows seven GroupBlocks of real operator work behind the bottom Disclosure.

findings.finding: The fold-1 real estate is spent entirely on read-only frames, and the page then repeats that same narrative a second time: ClientTrustSpinePreview re-renders the identical verdictTitle, valueAtStake, and recoveredSoFar the hero already showed, inserting ~1 fold of duplicate story between the moves queue and Operations.
findings.evidence: EngineSurface.tsx:640-714 (CommandCenterVerdict + value frame consume verdictTitle/moneyFrame) vs :971-985 (ClientTrustSpinePreview receives the same verdictTitle, formatMoney(moneyFrame.valueAtStake), formatMoney(moneyFrame.recoveredSoFar)). The preview itself is a protected gain (audit §7.7 'the artifact I always needed') — the issue is placement, not existence.

findings.finding: The post-sweep topbar is honest but meta-only: it carries Generate/Refresh/Send-N-staged with the always-rendered disabled Send + '0 staged — stage moves below to send' helper, which correctly names the dependency but then makes the operator scroll two folds to satisfy it — the helper text is a signpost to a destination with no road.
findings.evidence: EngineSurface.tsx:396-427 (topbar actions incl. sendHelperId text 'stage moves below to send'); uxflow-dataset.md headline 1: 'Topbar holds meta-actions only (Update/Regenerate/Refresh/Send-0-staged)'; prior audit B5 resolved for affordance-at-rest but not for distance-to-work.

recommendations.recommendation: QUICK WIN — render the missing jump-nav senders: add a compact anchor row to the existing opening Toolbar (EngineSurface.tsx:594) built from ENGINE_LENSES minus 'spine' (Changes · Signals · POV · Moves · Operations), each button calling state.setLens(id), and make that Toolbar sticky (position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: var(--z-sticky) from src/tokens.css). Use ghost Buttons or the Segmented primitive (src/components/ui/forms/Segmented.tsx, roving-tabindex built in) — no new state, no new receiver: the scroll+focus effect at EngineSurface.tsx:500-513 and the URL persistence in useEngineSurfaceState already do everything.
recommendations.rationale: This is the highest leverage-to-effort ratio on the surface: it converts every 2-3-fold scroll into one click, gives Operations a top-of-page entrance without moving it (respecting the V5 approved exception), and pays down the audit's 'URL lens vocabulary larger than visible UI' dead-code debt by finally rendering the labels ENGINE_LENSES has carried since build. It is arguably contract-consistent, not a contract change — engine-contract.md:92 already specifies '?lens= keeps the full spine rendered and focuses the corresponding section'; this just adds the visible sender half.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Navigation aid, not a cure: 'Stage for issue' still physically lives at y=1750, so the fold-1 view remains verb-free; adds one persistent row of chrome to a platform the audit already flags for lens/mode ceremony (~30 top-level options across 10 surfaces); sticky positioning needs a check against the approved 1180px spine geometry and the fixed CurationBulkActionBar so the two bars never stack.

recommendations.recommendation: OPTION A (recommended structural fix) — actions-forward spine reorder, pending owner circle-back: keep the opening cluster byte-identical (eyebrow Toolbar → StrategyDiff → nudges → CommandCenterVerdict → 4-tile value frame; the verdict-first hero is a protected gain), then move the work block (CurationMeter + NeedsAttentionStrip + BackingMovesQueue + EngineProjectionLenses, EngineSurface.tsx:884-969) directly after the value frame; follow with a 'compose the issue' pair (compact DraftedPovEditor card + ClientTrustSpinePreview adjacent, so draft-narrative and client-preview sit side by side in the send loop); fold StanceBar + IntelligenceSignals + LostQueryRecoveryCard into one collapsible 'Evidence behind this issue' Disclosure/GroupBlock (defaultOpen=false, stats=[{signals: N}], same ?lens=signals key-remount auto-open pattern the Operations Disclosure already uses at EngineSurface.tsx:997-999); Operations stays last per V5.
recommendations.rationale: Puts 'Stage for issue' at roughly y≈900-1000 (fold 1-1.5 instead of 2), which is what the owner literally asked for, and it improves the causal loop: stage moves → refresh POV → check the trust-spine preview → hit topbar Send reads top-to-bottom instead of ricocheting. Pairing POV with the preview also removes the duplicate-narrative dead zone (finding 5). The contract's circle-back trigger (engine-contract.md:135) fired on exactly this measure, so revisiting the section order is the anticipated path, not a rogue change.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Inverts the prototype's story order (contract §Required Interaction Model puts POV at 3, moves at 6) — an operator could stage moves before reading the narrative they are meant to back; requires an explicit ODP circle-back plus re-pinning the 'prototype opening order' assertions the Automated Test Floor locks (engine-contract.md:336); collapsing signals by default trades evidence-at-a-glance for depth (mitigated: Disclosure keeps it one click away and ?lens=signals still auto-opens); per CLAUDE.md protocol, run an evaluative persona pass before shipping since this is a structural reframe of a significant surface.

recommendations.recommendation: OPTION B (no-reorder alternative) — sticky curation tray: generalize the CurationBulkActionBar idiom (BackingMovesQueue.tsx:326-356 already ships a fixed-position bar outside the SectionCard) into a persistent staged-set tray that docks beneath the topbar once the user scrolls past the verdict: 'N staged · M need attention · [Review moves] [Operator tools]' where the buttons call state.setLens('moves'|'operations') to scroll — never a second Send (V2 approved the exact-once topbar Send; the tray points, the topbar commits). Combine with the Option-quick-win jump nav or fold the anchors into the tray itself.
recommendations.rationale: Zero change to the owner-approved section order and 1180px spine geometry — the contract stays untouched while the operator carries their work state with them: at any scroll depth the staged count, attention count, and a one-click path to the queue are visible. It also extends the platform's own proven pattern (the Keywords pilot's sticky bulk bar was one of the three real fixes the flag-ON smoke found) rather than inventing a new one.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Treats the symptom: the page is still 3.4 folds with the work at fold 2 — the tray makes the distance cheap, not short; two sticky layers (tray + CurationBulkActionBar when a bulk selection exists) need explicit stacking rules on the --z-sticky scale or they collide; adds standing chrome that fires the audit's overcomplication concern (Q5) and needs a visibility rule (only render when isRealStrategy && activeRecs.length > 0) so cold workspaces don't carry an empty bar.

recommendations.recommendation: OPTION C (lightest structural change) — collapse the narrative middle in place: keep the exact contract order but wrap StanceBar ('How we are spending the effort', EngineSurface.tsx:840-865) and the signals block (IntelligenceSignals + LostQueryRecoveryCard, :867-882) in collapsible GroupBlocks defaulting closed, each with count/summary meta (the EngineOperations GroupBlocks at EngineOperations.tsx:136-170 are the in-file template), auto-opened by ?lens=signals via the existing key-remount pattern. Net effect: 'Stage for issue' rises from y≈1750 to roughly y≈1000-1100 with zero section reordering. Pair with the quick-win jump nav for Operations reachability.
recommendations.rationale: Delivers most of Option A's fold-distance gain at a fraction of the approval cost: no section moves, so the parity contract's Required Interaction Model order (orientation → value → POV → stance → evidence → moves) is preserved textually — sections merely start collapsed, exactly as the owner-approved Operations Disclosure already does. Deep-link behavior (?lens=signals focuses and reveals) survives unchanged.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Weakest on the second inversion — Operations stays at the bottom (hence pairing with the jump nav); hiding stance + signals by default costs the daily operator the passive evidence glance the spine was designed for (the four-signal shortlist was itself a deliberate Wave-1 correction), and the 'plain-English narrative' gain the audit says to protect (§7.6) gets one Disclosure click gate; still needs light owner sign-off since the contract's test floor pins signal density and section presence.

recommendations.recommendation: Governance rider for whichever option is chosen: file the change as the ODP-001 circle-back it is — cite the dataset numbers (3.4 folds, 1/19 above fold, first primary @1750, operations @2940) as the trigger evidence in engine-contract.md, get explicit owner ratification of the new order or nav layer, update the pinned 'prototype opening order' assertions in the same PR, and re-run the 1440x900 fold/action instrumentation afterward as the acceptance measure (target: first primary verb ≤ fold 1.5, ≥5 in-page actions above fold, all six ENGINE_LENSES anchors reachable in one click).
recommendations.rationale: The Engine's current shape survived seven Sol review rounds and two owner approvals precisely because every change was contract-logged; an unlogged reorder would fork the contract from the code the way the audit's §1a ancestry confusion forked the branch history. Re-measuring with the same instrument that exposed the inversion converts 'feels better' into the same class of evidence that justified the change, and gives the next audit a before/after baseline.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Adds process latency to a change the owner already verbally endorsed; the fold-target numbers are advisory heuristics, not a contract — over-fitting to them could pressure future surfaces into cramming verbs above the fold at the expense of the verdict-first orientation that all seven personas praised.

judges.headline: Adopt one "bounded workbench" convention — verdict + toolbar pinned in a flex-none header, exactly ONE contained collection region (min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto) below it, and nothing ever rendered beneath the collection — the idiom the platform already ships twice (SEO Editor at 993px/1.1 folds, Page Intelligence at 900px/1.0 fold with 791 contained actions), applied in four calibers: pure containment for Site Audit, containment + row-cap + drawer-demotion for Schema, cap+expander for the Search & Traffic report table, and containment + load-more batching + drawer-demotion for Media.

findings.finding: Four rebuilt surfaces render unbounded collections directly into page flow while the correct containment idiom already exists twice in the same codebase. The fix is application of an existing pattern, not invention: SEO Editor bounds its workbench at viewport height (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:278-286, `height: calc(100vh - var(--shell-topbar) - var(--page-pad-y) - 24px)` + overflow-hidden) and gives its DataTable `h-full overflow-auto` inside a `min-h-0 flex-1` wrapper (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:535-542); Page Intelligence does the same with a master/detail grid whose list pane owns `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto` (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:233, :274, :290). Both pin search/sort/filter in the flex-none header. Page Intelligence proves action count is not the problem — 791 in-page actions in a 1.0-fold page — unbounded page flow is.
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md rows: Site Audit 42,486px / 47.2 folds; Schema 34,404px / 38.2 folds / 541 actions; Search & Traffic 32,096px / 35.7 folds; Media 24,702px / 27.4 folds / 1,583 actions — vs SEO Editor 993px/1.1 ('contained worksheet — the GOOD pattern') and Page Intelligence 900px/1.0/791 actions ('master/detail with internal scroll — the GOOD pattern'); dataset Headline pattern #2 states the containment idiom already ships.

findings.finding: Schema's 541 in-page actions come from 766 always-mounted FormSelect dropdowns — one per table row (SchemaPageTable.tsx:90-97) — that DUPLICATE an affordance the row's own drawer already has (SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:212 mounts the identical SCHEMA_PAGE_TYPE_OPTIONS FormSelect). The generated-pages table also has no in-region search or status filter: GeneratorLens.tsx:340 mounts a SearchField only in the pre-scan inventory state; once generation has run, the 766-row table is filterless.
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md: 'Schema (seo-schema) 34,404px, 38.2 folds, 541 actions, "Generate all pages" @362 (766-URL table unbounded)'; SchemaPageTable.tsx renders every page in `pages.map(...)` into a flow DataTable with a per-row FormSelect; SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:212 already has the same select.

findings.finding: Media's 1,583 interactive elements come from ~5 per asset card (open-preview ClickableRow, checkbox, name ClickableRow, Compress button, Alt-text-or-Rename button — AssetGrid.tsx:97-206) rendered for every filtered asset with no cap, and the per-card Compress/Alt/Rename buttons duplicate actions the AssetDrawer already implements (AssetDrawer.tsx onCompress/onSmartRename/alt editing) and the existing sticky bulk bar already covers for multi-select (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:1010-1044). The in-region toolbar (FilterChips + SearchField + sort + view, AssetManagerSurface.tsx:905-1000) is already built — only the bounding and DOM diet are missing.
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md: 'Media (asset-manager) 24,702px, 27.4 folds, 1,583 actions, Upload @326 (asset grid unbounded)'; AssetGrid.tsx:96 `grid ... {assets.map(...)}` with no slice/window; sticky bulk banner at AssetManagerSurface.tsx:1010 (`sticky top-0 z-[var(--z-dropdown)]`).

findings.finding: Content rendered BELOW the unbounded collections is effectively unreachable — stranded 27-47 folds down. Site Audit places the suppressed-rules card, AuditEvidence, the 'From fix to proof' narrative, and ActionItemsPanel after the issues table (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1026-1057); Schema places its production-support panels (SitePlanBridge, BusinessProfilePanel, CmsMappingPanel) after the 766-row table (GeneratorLens.tsx:530+); Media places the 'From media fix to proof' banner after the grid. Any containment convention must therefore also state the ordering rule: supporting content moves above the collection, into a Disclosure/GroupBlock, or into the item drawer — never below.
findings.evidence: SiteAuditSurface.tsx return order at :865-1057 (issues SectionCard at :974, four more sections after it); GeneratorLens.tsx SchemaPageTable at :519 followed by `schema-production-support` at :530; dataset fold depths make everything below the collections 27-47 folds deep.

findings.finding: Search & Traffic's canyon is a different genus: a read-only report table, not a worklist. The Detail ChartCard renders the server's default 500 query rows + 500 page rows wholesale (server/search-console.ts:226-227 `maxQueryRows = options.queryLimit || 500`; SearchLens.tsx:263-266 maps `data.overview.topQueries` with no slice; DataTable mounted at SearchLens.tsx:390-395 with no cap, search, or max-height). The page is otherwise a healthy narrative report (spark tiles → trend chart → Movement → Detail → context band → Disclosure), so full workbench conversion would be wrong — only the collection needs bounding. The prior audit already queued exactly this (§6 item 8: 'cap Search & Traffic's 500-row detail table').
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md: 'Search & Traffic 32,096px, 35.7 folds, 57 actions, uncapped 500-row GSC detail table in page flow' (audit finding #27); 2026-07-16-admin-ui-rebuild-vs-legacy-persona-audit.md §6 item 8.

findings.finding: Site Audit is the cheapest canyon: its issues SectionCard already contains the complete in-region toolbar the convention requires — SearchField + severity FilterChips + clear-filters (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:988-1006) and a 'Showing X of Y groups' count (:981-983) — and its rows are read-only (all actions live in IssueDetailDrawer, :1059). Only the bounding is missing: the whole surface stacks in a `space-y-[14px]` page-flow column (:866). Virtualization would be over-engineering here; DataTable's default stickyHeader (DataTable.tsx:64, :131) already works inside a scroll container.
findings.evidence: uxflow-dataset.md: 'Site Audit 42,486px, 47.2 folds, 24 actions, "Accept all 785" @764 (toolbar good; unbounded issue list below)' — note only 24 actions across 47 folds, confirming rows are passive.

findings.finding: The two good implementations have already drifted from each other, which is the argument for codifying the idiom as a DS layout primitive before the third and fourth copies are hand-rolled: SEO Editor's height calc ends `- 24px` (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:282) while Page Intelligence's ends `- var(--page-pad-bottom)` (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:233), and Page Intelligence needed a pr-check escape hatch comment (:232) to ship its bounded section.
findings.evidence: SeoEditorSurface.tsx:282 vs PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:233 — same intent, two hand-copied calc strings; pr-check-disable-next-line at PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:232.

recommendations.recommendation: Ratify the containment convention and write it into docs/rules/ui-rebuild-consistency.md as the collection-surface skeleton: (1) page root is a flex column bounded at `height: calc(100vh - var(--shell-topbar) - var(--page-pad-y) - var(--page-pad-bottom))` with overflow-hidden; (2) a `flex-none` pinned region holds the verdict hero (compact strip form), summary tiles, and ONE Toolbar carrying in-region SearchField/FilterChips/sort and the bulk-action band; (3) exactly one `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto` collection region (DataTable gets `h-full overflow-auto` + its default stickyHeader); (4) nothing renders below the collection — supporting panels move above it, into a Disclosure/GroupBlock, or into the item Drawer; (5) when rows carry mounted form controls or the set exceeds ~150, bound the DOM with an in-region cap ('Showing 100 of N — Show more') rather than virtualization.
recommendations.rationale: Both good examples (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:278-286 + SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:535-542; PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:233/:274/:290) already implement this exactly; naming it stops the four canyons from each inventing a different fix, and rule-doc placement matches how the @ds-rebuilt gates are governed.
recommendations.tradeoffs: A contained region sacrifices whole-page Cmd+F across the full list — the in-region SearchField is the mitigation and is therefore mandatory, not optional, wherever containment is applied.

recommendations.recommendation: Extract the idiom as a DS layout primitive — `WorkbenchFrame` in src/components/ui/layout/ (sibling of PageContainer/Toolbar/GroupBlock, exported from the ui barrel): props `pinned` (rendered flex-none) and `children` (rendered min-h-0 flex-1); the component owns the single canonical height-calc string and the overflow-hidden root. Migrate SEO Editor and Page Intelligence onto it in the same PR so there is one calc string, not two drifting ones.
recommendations.rationale: SeoEditorSurface.tsx:282 (`- 24px`) and PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:233 (`- var(--page-pad-bottom)`) already disagree; four more hand-copies are about to be written. A primitive also gives pr-check something greppable (@ds-rebuilt surfaces rendering >N-row collections outside a WorkbenchFrame) if the team later wants a mechanized gate.
recommendations.tradeoffs: One more layout primitive to maintain, and the F4 AppShell freeze means it must stay a page-content primitive, not shell chrome. Migrating the two good surfaces risks pixel diffs — the Keywords-pilot flag-ON smoke mechanism covers that.

recommendations.recommendation: Site Audit → pure contained scroll (the Page Intelligence caliber). Wrap the loaded-audit return (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:865) in the frame: pinned = compact AuditHero strip + CategoryCards (they double as category filters — keep them visible) + the actions Toolbar (:877-908) + the AI-fix bulk band (:914-945); contained = the existing issues SectionCard with its Toolbar (:988-1006) and DataTable (:1008-1022) given `h-full overflow-auto`. Relocate the four stranded below-table sections: suppressed-rules (:1026) becomes a 'Suppressed (N)' FilterChip/toggle in the issues toolbar; AuditEvidence and the 'From fix to proof' card (:1039-1053) move into a Disclosure above the collection; ActionItemsPanel (:1055) moves beside the bulk band or into the schedule drawer area. No pagination or virtualization — rows are passive (24 actions across 47.2 folds) and severity/category/search filters already subset the groups.
recommendations.rationale: This canyon is 47.2 folds (dataset) but is the cheapest fix: the in-region toolbar the convention requires is already built; only the bounding and the below-table relocations are missing. It also strengthens the prior audit's B1 concern — 'Accept all 785' and the issue queue stay on one screen with the verdict, so scope stays legible.
recommendations.tradeoffs: The hero + category cards + toolbar + bulk band consume ~450px of pinned height at 1440x900, leaving roughly half a fold for the table — the hero must compact (single-line verdict strip) or the category cards must collapse to chips, otherwise the containment just relocates the cramp.

recommendations.recommendation: Schema → contained scroll + in-region search/filter + drawer-demotion + row cap (the heaviest caliber). In GeneratorLens: pinned = readiness hero (:372-403) as a compact strip + SchemaWorkflowStrip + the 4 MetricTiles + bulk band (:449-494); contained = SchemaPageTable with a new in-region SearchField and status FilterChips (Needs fixes / Published / Stale / Retracted — validationStatusForPage/staleDaysForPage in schemaFormatters.ts already compute these). Inside SchemaPageTable, replace the per-row FormSelect (SchemaPageTable.tsx:90-97) with read-only text + tone Badge — the type edit already lives in SchemaPageDrawer (FormSelect at :212), reached by the existing onOpenPage row click — and cap rendered rows at 100 with a 'Show 100 more (N remaining)' expander. Move the production-support panels (GeneratorLens.tsx:530+) above the table as collapsible GroupBlocks or into the Workflow Guide lens.
recommendations.rationale: 541 of the dataset's actions are duplicate affordances: 766 mounted selects that repeat the drawer's own control. Demotion cuts the action surface ~50x and removes 766 listener-bearing form controls from the DOM; the cap bounds the remaining ~68px-min-height rows (766 rows ≈ 52,000px even inside a scroll region); search/status chips restore findability that Cmd+F loses.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Setting a type hint becomes two clicks (open drawer → select) instead of one — acceptable because type-hinting is a review-time act done per page, and the drawer is where validation context lives anyway. The cap means 'select-all-shown'-style semantics must be worded carefully (Site Audit's ':568 visible-vs-full-audit' footnote is the honest-labeling precedent).

recommendations.recommendation: Search & Traffic → cap + expander, NOT a workbench conversion (the report caliber). Keep the narrative page scroll (tiles → chart → Movement → Detail → context band → Disclosure). In the Detail ChartCard (SearchLens.tsx:372-396): default to `rows.slice(0, 50)` with the existing row-count label becoming 'Showing 50 of 500 rows'; 'Show all N' expands the DataTable inside a `max-h-[60vh] overflow-auto` container (stickyHeader is already DataTable's default); add an in-region SearchField filtering the query/page string so the 500 rows are searchable without scrolling. Same treatment for both Segmented modes (Queries/Pages).
recommendations.rationale: Dataset: 32,096px page where one read-only table contributes ~28k px; the prior audit's §6 item 8 already queued 'cap Search & Traffic's 500-row detail table'. A report page's scroll IS its reading model — the convention's unit of containment is the collection, not the page, and this surface is the proof case that keeps the convention honest.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Top-50 default hides long-tail rows behind one click; operators hunting a specific query get the in-region search instead. Cmd+F works fully only after 'Show all', which the max-h container then partially defeats — the search field is the primary path, so it must land in the same change, not as a follow-up.

recommendations.recommendation: Media → contained region + load-more batching + card DOM diet. Under the existing Toolbar (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:905-1000, already carrying FilterChips/search/sort/view): make the grid/table the contained `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto` region; render assets in 60-card batches with an in-region 'Load more (N remaining)' Button (grid keeps scan continuity better than numbered pagination; sort lives in the toolbar so pagination buys nothing). Slim AssetGrid cards (AssetGrid.tsx:161-202) to checkbox + open — demote Compress/Alt/Rename to the AssetDrawer (which already implements all three) and to the existing sticky bulk bar (:1010-1044) for multi-asset work; keep at most one contextual primary per card (Alt text when missing, since 'No alt' is the red badge driving the workflow). Move the 'From media fix to proof' banner above the grid or into the Repair-results (audit) lens; apply the same containment to AssetTable, the sibling view.
recommendations.rationale: 1,583 interactive elements (dataset) is the platform's largest DOM-size liability — ~300 cards x 5 controls plus 300 eager decodes on one page. 60-card batching + demotion cuts mounted interactive elements ~90% (to roughly 120-180) and bounds image decode pressure; the sticky bulk bar was built exactly for the multi-asset case the per-card buttons pretend to serve.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Single-asset compress becomes drawer-mediated (one extra click); if telemetry shows per-card compress is a high-frequency solo action, keep it as the one card button and demote only rename/alt. 'Select all shown' semantics must be re-worded against the 60-card window ('all loaded' vs 'all matching filter') to avoid a new truth-in-labels landmine — the exact class the just-merged sweep fixed.

judges.headline: The rebuild already contains its own best conventions — Page Intelligence/SEO Editor containment (900-993px, 1.0-1.1 folds), Keywords' window microcopy, Competitors' teach-with-CTA empty states — but nothing enforces them, so sibling surfaces ship 27-47-fold scroll canyons, three-way nav naming, four stacked filter layers, and 54/87 actionless empty states; the fix is codifying ~8 measurable usability budgets, each with a named enforcement home (contract test, pr-check rule, fold-instrumentation script, or checklist), not inventing new patterns.

findings.finding: Four surfaces blow any plausible depth budget by 10-50x because unbounded collections render in page flow — while the repo already ships two containment idioms it is not reusing, and the DS DataTable primitive offers no vertical containment mode to opt into.
findings.evidence: Dataset: Site Audit 42,486px / 47.2 folds; Schema 34,404px / 38.2 folds / 541 in-page actions (766-URL table); Search & Traffic 32,096px / 35.7 folds (uncapped 500-row GSC detail table); Media 24,702px / 27.4 folds / 1,583 actions. The good idioms are in-repo: SEO Editor 993px/1.1 folds (bounded worksheet) and Page Intelligence 900px flat with 791 actions via master/detail internal scroll (src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:290 `overflow-y-auto`). src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx supports only overflow-x (line 122) and client-side header sort — no maxHeight/initialRows/virtualization, so surfaces cannot contain a collection without hand-rolling.

findings.finding: Naming parity (sidebar = breadcrumb = palette = in-surface title) is violated three-to-four ways for the same destinations, and it is the single most mechanically checkable budget — yet no contract test pins it.
findings.evidence: RebuiltSidebar GROUP_PRESENTATION hard-codes label overrides 'Insights Engine' / 'Keywords' / 'Asset Manager' (src/components/layout/RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-127) while the registry that drives breadcrumb + document.title says 'Strategy' / 'Keyword Hub' / 'Assets' (src/lib/navRegistry.tsx:130-136); CommandPalette groups by legacy zone names 'Monitoring' / 'Site Health' (src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:46-47). A fourth vocabulary lives inside the Engine itself: its send header is titled 'The Issue' (src/components/strategy/issue/IssueHeader.tsx:82). Prior audit B6 confirmed; still true on post-sweep staging @66fef654f.

findings.finding: Keywords stacks four control layers over one dataset, and code confirms two of its five lenses are the same table in disguise — Opportunities is literally a column array swap plus a default sort, and Pages/Clusters are group-by presentations of the current 50-row page that need a warning banner to stay honest. Meanwhile the genuinely actionable 'Client keyword feedback' block sits below the 50-row table at y≈3411.
findings.evidence: Layer stack in src/components/keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsSurface.tsx: LensSwitcher (5 lenses, :377-390) + search/'More filters' toolbar (:393-411) + 7 status FilterChips (:413-423) + a second FilterChip row inside the table doing SORT, disambiguated only by a leading 'Sort' label (src/components/keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsTable.tsx:484-501 — the comment admits the same primitive serves two axes). Opportunities lens: `activeColumns = state.lens === 'opportunities' ? opportunityColumns : columns` (KeywordsTable.tsx:480) + default sort opportunity/desc (useKeywordsSurfaceState.ts:101-104). Pages/Clusters group only the current page (KeywordsLenses.tsx:266-279 truncation banner: 'these lenses group the current page, not the full set'). Feedback block: KeywordsSurface.tsx:430-463; dataset note 5 places it at y≈3411. Dataset: Keywords = 22 in-page actions, 3.9 folds (depth is table content, placement is healthy).

findings.finding: 54 of 87 EmptyState mounts across rebuilt surfaces carry no action, and the wording splits into teachers vs apologizers: Search & Traffic is the worst offender with 23 EmptyStates, zero actions, and ~17 provider-shrug dead-ends ('GA4 did not return source rows'), while the exact same conditions get the correct teach-with-CTA treatment elsewhere — the platform has the pattern, it just isn't a convention.
findings.evidence: Brace-balanced census: 87 total, 33 with action, 54 without. Apologizers: TrafficLens.tsx:100-101 ('No GA4 data — GA4 did not return overview rows for this workspace and window'), TrafficLens.tsx:211,228, SearchLens.tsx:231-232,347,394, BreakdownsDrawer.tsx (7 instances, all 'did not return X rows'). Even setup states name the destination without linking it: TrafficLens.tsx:79-80 and SearchLens.tsx:207-208 say 'Connect ... in Workspace Settings' with no button, while CompetitorsSurface.tsx:228-244 ships the same condition WITH 'Open Workspace Settings' CTA. Connect-a-site states missing CTA: LinksSurface.tsx:259, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:219, AssetManagerSurface.tsx:783, SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1217. Filter-empty without clear button: RoadmapBacklog.tsx:17, RoadmapSprintGroups.tsx:18, SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1018, AnnotationsLens.tsx:292, LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:627. Teachers to protect: OutcomeRecentWins.tsx:71-72, DiagnosticsListView.tsx:55-57 (names condition AND links the source surface), ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:168-170.

findings.finding: In-surface jargon an operator would not say aloud on a client call, concentrated in the dense trio (Engine, Keywords, Cockpit work queue) — coined nouns shipped without lexicon entries or plain-language fallbacks.
findings.evidence: 'Backing moves live' as a stat label (src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:308,697); 'Stage for issue' per-row verb (src/components/strategy/issue/BackingMovesQueue.tsx:36) and 'Send N staged' (EngineSurface.tsx:423); stream key 'unclassified' leaking into UI copy — group title 'Risk and unclassified', description 'Client signals and unclassified attention.' (src/components/cockpit-rebuilt/CockpitWorkQueue.tsx:66-70; src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineWorkQueue.tsx:64-67); 'Raw Evidence' as a lifecycle badge label (src/components/ui/statusConfig.ts:170); 'Money' stream with unit 'to pitch' and description 'Value and pricing work with real provenance' (CockpitWorkQueue.tsx:57-61, EngineWorkQueue.tsx:56-58); 'proof pending' badge (EngineSurface.tsx:273,687); 'Unclustered keywords' group title (KeywordsLenses.tsx:141); column headers 'Opp' and single-char '$' (KeywordsTable.tsx:326,348). Counter-example done right on the same surface: the stance section is titled 'How we are spending the effort' (EngineSurface.tsx:847) — plain language over 'stance'.

findings.finding: The Guide idiom ships three ways on three surfaces, and 'Content Health' names two different mounts of the same underlying decay view with no cross-reference — both confirmed in code, both cheap convention fixes.
findings.evidence: Guide as Drawer: ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:419,562 ('Content Pipeline Guide'); Guide as tab/lens: PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:267 (`activeTab === 'guide'`); Guide as Disclosure: SiteAuditSurface.tsx:640 ('Audit Guide'). 'Content Health': a Content Pipeline lens counting decay (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:92) AND a Site Audit Disclosure mounting ContentDecay (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:627) — plus Engine deep-links to `content-pipeline?tab=content-health` (EngineSurface.tsx:438), so the term is load-bearing in URLs too.

findings.finding: The ⌘K palette is navigation-only (zero action verbs for a tool whose surfaces each have one primary verb) and lists debug workspaces alongside real clients — a convention gap and a trust landmine for a live-call operator.
findings.evidence: Dataset headline 4: 'admin destinations + workspace switcher; zero action verbs (no run-audit/send/regenerate/stage). Palette also lists debug workspaces (cascade-debug-*, dbg*, Trigger Check WS) alongside real clients.' Palette code groups by legacy zones only (src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:40-47,144).

findings.finding: Engine remains the unique action-inversion after the trust sweep: the always-rendered disabled send with '0 staged — stage moves below to send' fixed discoverability (audit B5), but the operative per-rec verbs still start two folds down and the operator tools sit at the literal page bottom; the Outcomes surface has the sibling inversion (data-entry form above the daily readback).
findings.evidence: Dataset: Engine first primary 'Stage for issue' @1750 (fold=900), 1 of 19 in-page actions above the fold, 'Setup and operator tools' @~2940 (string at src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:1003). Sweep improvement verified in code: EngineSurface.tsx:407-424 renders the helper + disabled primary send at rest. Outcomes inversion: dataset qualitative note 'leads with the Record-published-work form ABOVE its readback tabs (inversion, audit-confirmed)'.

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt an 8-budget 'usability budget' contract for every current and future admin surface, each with a named enforcement home: (1) PRIMARY ACTION ≤ 1 FOLD — first enabled primary-variant button at y<900 @1440x900 on representative data, or the surface explicitly declares itself verdict-only → enforce via a checked-in fold-instrumentation Playwright script (productionize the exact methodology of uxflow-dataset.md as tests/e2e/fold-budget.spec.ts with per-surface assertions) + a pr-readiness-checklist line; (2) DEPTH ≤ 4 FOLDS / COLLECTIONS CONTAINED — any collection >50 rows paginates, goes master/detail with internal scroll, or virtualizes → same instrumentation asserts pageHeight; (3) ONE IDIOM PER PATTERN — a lens = different mental model only (column preset ≠ lens, group-by ≠ lens); max 2 control rows above a collection; Guide = Drawer everywhere → review checklist + evaluative persona pass, documented in docs/workflows/use-primitives.md; (4) EVERY EMPTY STATE NAMES ITS NEXT ACTION — action prop, or description naming the populating condition or the exact fix location → pr-check customCheck on src/components/*-rebuilt: <EmptyState> with neither action nor description fails; wording quality ('did not return rows' = apology) stays a checklist item; (5) EVERY AGGREGATE STATES ITS WINDOW/BASIS — FreshnessStamp/ProvenanceChip/caption using shared constants (the KeywordsTable.tsx:513-515 microcopy + GSC_METRIC_WINDOW_DAYS import is the reference implementation) → pr-check regex forbidding hard-coded window literals ('last 28 days', 'Rolling 90') in *-rebuilt outside shared constants; (6) SIDEBAR=BREADCRUMB=PALETTE=TITLE NAMING → contract test (tests/contract/nav-naming-parity.test.ts) importing GROUP_PRESENTATION, NAV_ENTRIES, and PALETTE_GROUP_LABELS and asserting one label per Page id; (7) VOCABULARY FROM THE REGISTRY — any new operator-facing coinage requires a GLOSSARY + shared/types/lexicon.ts entry in the same commit (existing verify:lexicon is the home; extend intake to UI copy); (8) READBACK ABOVE DATA-ENTRY — forms mount below the verdict/readback or in a Drawer on reading surfaces → checklist + persona audit.
recommendations.rationale: Every confirmed convention failure in the dataset is a budget violation the platform elsewhere passes — the problem is not missing patterns but missing enforcement. Budgets 4, 5, and 6 are deterministic and cheap to mechanize today (this codebase already runs 100+ pr-check rules, contract-test censuses, and ratchets — the enforcement machinery exists); budgets 1-3 and 8 need a browser or a human, which is exactly what the fold-instrumentation script and the persona-audit cadence provide.
recommendations.tradeoffs: The fold-instrumentation e2e needs seeded representative data and will be the flakiest gate — run it in the manual-only / push-CI tier per docs/rules/verification-governance.md rather than PR CI. Budget 3 (one idiom) resists full mechanization; accepting it as a checklist item means it depends on review discipline.

recommendations.recommendation: Keywords filter-layer diet, validated against the code: collapse the lens tray from 5 to 2 (Rankings table + Lifecycle board). Fold Opportunities into a 'Columns: Full | Triage' Segmented in the table Toolbar — it is ALREADY just a column-array swap (KeywordsTable.tsx:480) plus a default sort (useKeywordsSurfaceState.ts:101-104); keep `?lens=opportunities` as a URL alias so existing deep-links (e.g. EngineSurface rank_drop handoff) keep working. Fold Pages/Clusters into a 'Group by: None | Page | Cluster' Segmented on the table — they already group only the current page (KeywordsLenses.tsx:266-279), and as an explicit table grouping the pagination controls stay visible, which retires the 'Grouped from the first 50 keywords' warning banner as primary UX (server-side grouping remains the DEF-kw follow-up already noted at KeywordsSurface.tsx:74-84). Kill the fourth layer entirely by adding controlled-sort props (sortState/onSortChange) to DataTable — it already has sortable headers with aria-sort (DataTable.tsx:19-20,71-80) but only client-side; wiring them to state.setSort deletes the 'Sort' FilterChip row and ends the same-primitive-two-axes ambiguity the code comment apologizes for (KeywordsTable.tsx:485-487). Finally, hoist the 'Client keyword feedback' GroupBlock (already collapsible, KeywordsSurface.tsx:430-463) above the table whenever requested-count > 0 — a human is waiting; it should not live at y≈3411.
recommendations.rationale: The dataset shows Keywords' depth is content, not misplacement — its real cost is choice ceremony: 5 lenses + 7 filter chips + 5 sort chips + a More-filters select over ONE dataset, where two lenses are non-distinct (prior audit §5 and §6 item 7). Because Opportunities and Pages/Clusters are already implemented as presets of the same query, the diet is a UI re-labeling of existing state machinery, not a data-model change — low risk, high ceremony reduction (visible top-level options drop from ~17 to ~11).
recommendations.tradeoffs: Lens counts in the tray (LensSwitcher count badges) go away for the folded lenses — acceptable since the code admits four of five preview the same counts.total anyway (KeywordsSurface.tsx:74-84). URL-alias handling for retired lens values must be kept one release to honor bookmarks. Controlled DataTable sort is a DS API addition that F3 primitive consumers should review (one new optional prop pair, backward compatible).

recommendations.recommendation: Vocabulary pass on the dense trio — concrete replacements, registered in docs/workflows/ui-vocabulary.md and the lexicon in the same commit: 'Backing moves live' → 'Moves in progress' (EngineSurface.tsx:308,697); 'Stage for issue' → 'Add to client update' and 'Send N staged' → 'Send client update (N)' (BackingMovesQueue.tsx:36, EngineSurface.tsx:423); 'Risk and unclassified' group + 'Client signals and unclassified attention.' → 'Needs triage' / 'Client signals and anything not yet sorted' (CockpitWorkQueue.tsx:66-70, EngineWorkQueue.tsx:64-67 — never surface the enum key); 'Raw Evidence' badge → 'Seen in search' (statusConfig.ts:170); 'Money' stream unit 'to pitch' + 'Value and pricing work with real provenance' → 'Growth' / 'to propose' / 'Upsell and value-proof work backed by measured results' (CockpitWorkQueue.tsx:57-61); 'proof pending' → 'measuring' (EngineSurface.tsx:273,687); 'Unclustered keywords' → 'Not in a topic yet' (KeywordsLenses.tsx:141); column header 'Opp' → 'Opportunity' in the Triage preset where width allows, '$' → '$/mo' (KeywordsTable.tsx:326,348; keep 'KD' — industry standard). Keep 'How we are spending the effort' (EngineSurface.tsx:847) as the house style exemplar. Also resolve the two-meanings collision: rename the Site Audit disclosure 'Content Health' (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:627) to 'Decaying pages' with a 'View in Content Pipeline' link, leaving Content Pipeline as the canonical 'Content Health' home (its ?tab=content-health deep-link contract stays intact).
recommendations.rationale: The persona audit's junior-SEO and account-manager seats both fail on exactly these strings ('the labels stop meaning what they say'); an operator narrating the screen on a client call cannot say 'backing moves live: 3' or 'this keyword is raw evidence'. The say-it-aloud test is the cheapest trust repair available, and the platform already has the locked-copy machinery (clientActionLabel/evergreenCopy pattern) to pin the results.
recommendations.tradeoffs: 'Issue'/'staged'/'moves' vocabulary is load-bearing in tests, testids, and the client-facing Issue feed — rename display strings only, not identifiers or WS events. Each rename needs the CLAUDE.md string-literal-rename grep discipline; budget one PR for the sweep rather than piecemeal.

recommendations.recommendation: Empty-state repair sweep, then the gate: (1) copy the CompetitorsSurface teach-with-CTA pattern (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:228-244) into the six connect/configure states that name a destination without linking it — TrafficLens.tsx:79 (GA4), SearchLens.tsx:207 (GSC), LinksSurface.tsx:259, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:219, AssetManagerSurface.tsx:783, SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1217 — each gets an 'Open Workspace Settings' (adminPath workspace-settings ?tab=connections) button; (2) add clear-filter buttons to the five filter-empty states missing them (RoadmapBacklog.tsx:17, RoadmapSprintGroups.tsx:18, SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1018, AnnotationsLens.tsx:292, LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:627) — the sibling surfaces (KeywordsTable.tsx:230-243, SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:460-462) already do; (3) rewrite the ~17 Search & Traffic provider-shrug descriptions from 'GA4/Search Console did not return X rows' to name the recoverable lever: 'Try a longer date range, or check the GA4 property in Workspace Settings' (TrafficLens, SearchLens, BreakdownsDrawer); (4) land the pr-check customCheck from budget 4 so the 54/87 actionless count can only ratchet down, and grandfather intentional teach-without-button states (OutcomeRecentWins.tsx:71, LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:289 — they name the populating condition, which passes the budget).
recommendations.rationale: The dataset and prior audit both praise 'empty states teach instead of apologize' as a rebuild gain — but the census shows that gain is only 38% enforced (33/87 with action) and one flagship surface (Search & Traffic, 35.7 folds deep) delivers 23 consecutive dead-ends. Every fix is a copy-paste of an in-repo pattern; the pr-check rule makes the convention permanent.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Inner-panel empties (BreakdownsDrawer's per-panel rows, PageWeightLens.tsx:366) arguably don't each need a button — the rule should require action OR condition-naming description, not force button spam into seven drawer panels. The wording rewrite touches copy asserted by component tests; grep for the strings first.

recommendations.recommendation: Nav-naming closure (budget 6 executed): owner picks one name per contested surface — 'Insights Engine' vs 'Strategy' (seo-strategy), 'Keywords' vs 'Keyword Hub' (seo-keywords), 'Asset Manager' vs 'Assets' (media) — then move the chosen label into src/lib/navRegistry.tsx and delete the GROUP_PRESENTATION label overrides in RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-127 so sidebar, breadcrumb, and document.title read the registry; re-key CommandPalette group sub-labels off the rebuilt sidebar groups ('Strategy & Content', 'Search & Site Health', 'Optimization') instead of legacy zones (CommandPalette.tsx:46-47); filter debug workspaces (cascade-debug-*, dbg*, Trigger Check WS) from the palette workspace switcher or badge them 'debug'; and pin all of it with tests/contract/nav-naming-parity.test.ts asserting no presentation override diverges from the registry label. Fold in the palette-verbs gap as a follow-up: register each surface's one primary verb (run audit, send client update, regenerate strategy, upload) as palette actions so ⌘K stops being navigation-only (dataset headline 4).
recommendations.rationale: This is prior-audit B6 plus dataset headline 4, and it is the highest trust-per-hour item in the queue: a new hire's first distrust trigger, mechanically preventable with a ~40-line contract test once the overrides are deleted. The registry already propagates to Sidebar/CommandPalette/Breadcrumbs by design (W3.4) — the overrides are the only leak.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Renaming 'Strategy' → 'Insights Engine' (or the reverse) touches the route-removal-checklist surface set (nav literals, tests asserting labels, docs). Palette action verbs are the larger half — they need per-surface wiring and confirm affordances for destructive verbs; ship naming parity first, verbs second.

recommendations.recommendation: Containment retrofits for the four scroll canyons using only existing idioms: add a contained mode to DataTable (maxHeight + internal scroll, or initialRows={50} with a truthful 'Show all N' expander) as the DS enabler; then (a) Schema's 766-URL table and (b) Media's asset grid adopt Page Intelligence's master/detail internal-scroll pattern (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:290), (c) Search & Traffic's 500-row GSC detail table gets initialRows + expander (prior audit §6 item 8 already queues this), and (d) Site Audit's unbounded issue list below its (healthy) toolbar paginates by issue group. Assert the result with the budget-2 fold instrumentation: all four surfaces ≤4 folds on the Expero dataset.
recommendations.rationale: Dataset headline 2 is explicit that 'the fix is applying an existing idiom, not inventing one' — Page Intelligence proves a 791-action surface fits in 900px flat. Schema at 38.2 folds with 541 actions and Media at 27.4 folds with 1,583 actions are unusable at scale and dwarf every other convention cost in the dataset; a single DataTable prop unlocks three of the four fixes.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Internal-scroll regions must preserve keyboard nav and the sticky bulk-bar patterns (the Keywords sticky selection bar depends on the AppShell scroll container — KeywordsTable.tsx:535-546 comment); test at mobile breakpoints where nested scroll is hostile. 'Show all' expanders must keep counts truthful (the display-cap banner pattern at KeywordsTable.tsx:517-526 is the honest template). Media may ultimately want virtualization, which is a bigger DS lift — pagination first.

judges.headline: Five of six daily journeys are 3–5 clicks on paper but pay a hidden "re-find tax" at cross-surface hops: Cockpit handoffs drop item identity into unbounded page flows (Site Audit 47.2 folds, Search & Traffic 35.7), the Engine hides its primary staging verbs two folds down behind a 6-lens URL vocabulary that has no visible switcher, and Requests answers open on the wrong tab with no nav badge — while SEO Editor's 1.1-fold worksheet proves the platform already owns the cheap pattern. Two task premises need correcting: Requests/Action Results DO have labeled sidebar homes on the current tip (RebuiltSidebar CLIENT-FACING group, shipped in #1519), and ⌘K is not literally navigation-only — it has 7 "actions," but 5 of them are navigation dressed as verbs, which is the same truth-in-labels defect the sweep just fixed elsewhere.

findings.finding: J1 Morning triage (Cockpit → act on top item) is cheap locally (0 scroll to queue, 1 click) but the hop loses item identity, so the true cost is 3–6+ interactions dominated by an unbounded re-find step on the destination surface. Sequence: land on Cockpit (1.3 folds, 16 actions above fold — healthy) → read verdict → click row 'Open'/'Review' (1) → surface hop → RE-FIND the item (search/scroll/filter, unbounded) → act (1). Cockpit's handleOpenQueueItem navigates to bare surface roots: rank_drop → Keywords root with NO ?lens=rankings, audit_error → Site Audit root (a 47.2-fold page), setup_gap → workspace-settings with NO ?tab=connections — while EngineSurface's twin handler for the SAME source types passes ?lens=rankings and ?tab=connections. The primary triage host has the weakest handoffs in the codebase.
findings.evidence: src/components/cockpit-rebuilt/CockpitSurface.tsx:75-86 (routes map, no lens/focus params) and :155-183 (handleOpenQueueItem) vs src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:430-454 (rank_drop → seo-keywords?lens=rankings, setup_gap → workspace-settings?tab=connections). Dataset: Cockpit 1,203px/1.3 folds; hop destinations Site Audit 42,486px/47.2 folds, Keywords 3,498px/3.9 folds, Search & Traffic 32,096px/35.7 folds.

findings.finding: J3 Stage + send a strategy update (Engine) — the surface's primary operator verb sits two folds below read-first material with no jump affordance, despite a fully wired URL remedy. Sequence: sidebar 'Insights Engine' (1) → scroll past StrategyDiff → staleness nudges → verdict → 4-tile value frame → POV → stance bar → signals to reach BackingMovesQueue ('Stage for issue' @y1750, the ONLY primary action and only 1 of 19 in-page actions above the fold) → stage N recs (N clicks; bulk Stage exists) → 'Send N staged' in the FIXED topbar (1 click — post-sweep the send button is always rendered, disabled at 0 staged with a '0 staged — stage moves below to send' helper, so B5's round-trip is closed). The expensive hop is the 2-fold scroll: ENGINE_LENSES defines 6 lenses (spine/changes/signals/pov/moves/operations), EngineSurface has a scroll-into-view receiver for ?lens=, yet NO visible lens switcher renders — the sole setLens consumer is the internal attention-strip handler. Dead URL vocabulary where a one-click jump nav should be.
findings.evidence: Dataset: Engine 3,033px/3.4 folds, 1/19 actions above fold, first primary 'Stage for issue' @1750. src/components/engine-rebuilt/useEngineSurfaceState.ts:8-15 (ENGINE_LENSES), EngineSurface.tsx:500-506 (?lens= scrollIntoView receiver), :457-459 (only setLens consumer), :594-960 (section order), git 66fef654f PR-3 diff (always-rendered disabled send + helper, EngineSurface.tsx:405-425). src/components/strategy/issue/BackingMovesQueue.tsx:36 (STAGE_ROW_LABEL).

findings.finding: J2 Repair an audit issue + notify the client is the best-co-located action journey once the issue is found — sidebar (1) → filter/search (1) → row click opens IssueDetailDrawer (1) → 'Accept suggestion' (1) → optional note + 'Send to client' (1) = ~5 clicks, repair and notify in one drawer with a toast. Two expensive residues: (a) the drawer acts on the FIRST affected page only (now honestly labeled 'Applies to {path} only' post-sweep), and the other instances in the drawer offer only Hide / Hide pattern — repairing a 14-page group costs 14 full drawer round-trips with no per-instance Accept/Send row and no 'apply to all N'; (b) locating a known issue means operating inside a 47.2-fold page flow when the platform's own master/detail internal-scroll idiom (Page Intelligence, 900px/1.0 fold with 791 actions) already solves this.
findings.evidence: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:436-521 (drawer footer + Accept suggestion + Send to client + note), :487 ('Applies to {firstPagePath} only'), :528-561 (instance rows expose only Hide/Hide pattern), :992-1006 (search + severity chips). Dataset: Site Audit 42,486px/47.2 folds vs Page Intelligence 900px/1.0 fold master/detail — 'the GOOD pattern'. Prior audit §5 validated 'Site Audit per-page repair depth' as the top major loss.

findings.finding: J5 Read + answer a client reply opens on the wrong tab and has no nav-level signal. Sequence: sidebar 'Requests' (1 — labeled, see correction below) → lands on DEFAULT tab 'Deliverables', not the conversational one → click 'All requests' (1) → expand the request thread (1) → scroll thread → type + Send note (1) = 4 clicks. Frictions: (a) 'the client sent something back' is split across 3 of 4 tabs (Deliverables/Signals/All requests/Client actions) with no unified recency-ordered inbox — which tab holds the reply is guesswork; (b) the ONLY badge wired in the rebuilt sidebar is pendingContentRequests on the Content Pipeline item, and the group-collapse badge fallback is also content-pipeline-only — so a waiting human never surfaces on the Requests nav item, and if the operator has collapsed CLIENT-FACING, the item (and any future badge) disappears entirely; discovery falls back to the icon-only footer NotificationBell or a Cockpit 'request' queue row (which does deep-link correctly to ?tab=requests). (c) All four tab panes are legacy components mounted wholesale (RequestManager, AdminInbox, ClientActionsTab).
findings.evidence: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/RequestsLens.tsx:111-117 (default 'deliverables', four legacy panes), :141 (invalid tab → 'opened Deliverables'); src/components/layout/RebuiltSidebar.tsx:403-405 (collapse badge only for groups containing content-pipeline) and :423 (item badge only on content-pipeline); CockpitSurface.tsx:82 (requests deep-link ?tab=requests); src/components/RequestManager.tsx:508-530 (thread reply composer).

findings.finding: J6 'Did our work land?' pays a data-entry toll before the readback: sidebar 'Action Results' (1, labeled) → OutcomeDashboard renders the RecordPublishedWorkCard FORM above the TabBar, so the daily readback (Top Wins default, Scorecard etc.) starts below an occasional-use form — the exact hierarchy inversion the dataset and prior audit flagged. Worse, the outcome tabs are deliberately NOT ?tab= deep-linkable (inline 'tab-deeplink-ok' waiver), contradicting the rebuild's own flagship gain (#1: URL-persisted state everywhere) and blocking Cockpit/⌘K from linking straight to Scorecard or Wins. Book-level readback (ADMIN → 'Team Outcomes') is 1 click and post-sweep honest ('All-time value · 28-day clicks' replaced the false 'Rolling 90 days'), but every visit carries a permanent 'Book totals are not yet available' info banner and attention-first sorting remains demoted behind per-row expansion.
findings.evidence: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeDashboard.tsx:40-42 (RecordPublishedWorkCard above TabBar; 'tab-deeplink-ok — outcomes tabs are not navigated to via ?tab='), :22 (default 'wins'); src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:43 (honest window label, post-sweep 66fef654f), :60-65 (permanent banner). Dataset earlier-walk note: 'Outcomes/Action Results leads with the Record-published-work form ABOVE its readback tabs (inversion, audit-confirmed)'.

findings.finding: J4 Fix titles/metas at scale (SEO Editor) is the reference-cheap journey — sidebar (1) → contained 1.1-fold worksheet, filters + bulk 'Draft missing titles/meta' at top (1) → inline edits → group/row checkboxes (1) → floating bulk bar send (1) ≈ 4 clicks, and the sweep closed the silent CMS send (worksheet-level success/error toasts). Two residual costs: (a) the bulk bar splits into 'Send static to client' AND 'Send CMS to client' — one logical 'send this week's meta fixes' on a mixed selection is two sends and two approval batches; (b) anything beyond title/meta (extra SEO fields, per-page detail) costs a SeoEditorPagePanel drawer round-trip per page, and the audit-validated CMS depth gaps (fields beyond the first, full bulk-preview diff) still cap the 'at scale' half of the journey.
findings.evidence: Dataset: SEO Editor 993px/1.1 folds, 12/12 actions above fold — 'the GOOD pattern'. src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:488-513 (selection bar, two send buttons), git 66fef654f PR-2 diff (approvalError/approvalSent toasts); SeoEditorSurface.tsx:249-256 (Refresh + Publish site topbar), :369-388 (bulk draft buttons); SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx (618-line per-page drawer). Prior audit §5: 4 validated SEO Editor CMS majors.

findings.finding: ⌘K is effectively navigation-only, but not for the reason the dataset states — the palette ships 7 workspace-scoped 'actions', of which only 2 dispatch real work (Run Audit via startJob('seo-audit'); Scan All Workspaces for Anomalies via anomalies.scan()). The other 5 ('Generate Schema', 'Create Brief', 'Create Content Template', 'Build Content Matrix', 'View Content Plan') only navigate — the same navigate-only-verb pattern the trust sweep just renamed on Cockpit rows. Compounding it, the palette renders registry labels ('Strategy', 'Keyword Hub', 'Assets', 'Pipeline') under legacy zone headers ('Monitoring', 'Site Health') while the sidebar the user just left says 'Insights Engine', 'Keywords', 'Asset Manager', 'Content Pipeline' — the triple taxonomy strikes mid-journey, and fuzzy-searching the sidebar's name can miss. Debug workspaces (cascade-debug-*, dbg*) list alongside real clients (dataset pattern 4).
findings.evidence: src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:167-254 (action items; :201, :209, :233, :241, :252 are pure navigate), :44-53 (PALETTE_GROUP_LABELS legacy zones), :140-152 (registry labels — only 'home' has labelWhenOn:'Cockpit' in navRegistry.tsx:114-116); RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-161 (GROUP_PRESENTATION label overrides: 'Insights Engine', 'Keywords', 'Asset Manager'). Dataset headline pattern 4.

findings.finding: Cross-journey: four scroll canyons sit exactly where journeys 1/2/4 hop into — Site Audit 47.2 folds, Schema 38.2, Search & Traffic 35.7, Media 27.4 all render unbounded collections into page flow, so every Cockpit/Engine handoff into them converts a 1-click hop into an unbounded scroll/search. The platform already ships both containment idioms (SEO Editor bounded worksheet; Page Intelligence master/detail with internal scroll) — this is application of an existing pattern, not new design.
findings.evidence: Dataset table rows: seo-audit 42,486px/47.2; seo-schema 34,404px/38.2/541 actions; analytics-hub 32,096px/35.7 (uncapped 500-row GSC table); asset-manager 24,702px/27.4/1,583 actions; vs seo-editor 993px and page-intelligence 900px. Dataset headline pattern 2; prior audit §6 item 8 (cap Search & Traffic's 500-row table).

findings.finding: CORRECTION to a task premise / stale audit claim: Requests and Action Results are NOT unlabeled footer icons on the current tip — RebuiltSidebar ships a labeled CLIENT-FACING group (Action Results, Requests) and an ADMIN group (Team Outcomes, Prospect, AI Usage, Roadmap, Features, Diagnostics), unchanged since #1519 which the 2026-07-16 audit tree already contained. The icon-only footer demotes only NotificationBell, Revenue, Settings, theme, and logout. Recommendations should target the RESIDUAL nav frictions — the missing Requests badge, the collapse-hides-group behavior, and the sidebar-vs-registry-vs-palette naming split — not re-add labeled homes that exist.
findings.evidence: src/components/layout/RebuiltSidebar.tsx:139-160 (CLIENT-FACING + ADMIN groups), :449-501 (footer icon strip = bell/revenue/settings/theme/logout); git log --follow RebuiltSidebar.tsx → last change f4278712f (#1519, 2026-07-12); prior audit B6 claimed 'Requests/Action Results/Diagnostics/Roadmap unlabeled' against staging @5a2641b21 which includes #1519 — stale as stated.

findings.finding: Engine's bottom-of-page 'Setup and operator tools' disclosure (y≈2940, the literal last element) buries a second full copy of the shared operator work queue plus Weekly Briefings, recommendation history, conversion connections, and client signal intake. An operator who triages from the Engine (its queue handler has the BETTER handoffs, finding J1) must scroll 3.4 folds and expand a disclosure to reach the same queue Cockpit shows above the fold — duplicate surface, inverted placement.
findings.evidence: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:990-1032 (Disclosure 'Setup and operator tools' wrapping EngineOperations + EngineWorkQueue as the final section); EngineOperations.tsx titles (Weekly Briefings :126, Recommendation history :138, Setup and handoffs :171, Client signal intake :275). Dataset headline 1: 'Setup and operator tools at the literal page bottom (y≈2940)'.

recommendations.recommendation: Carry item identity through every Cockpit queue hop: adopt EngineSurface's handler as the canonical one (rank_drop → seo-keywords?lens=rankings, setup_gap → workspace-settings?tab=connections) and extend the platform's existing two-halves ?tab= contract with a ?focus=<itemRef> receiver on the two canyon destinations — Site Audit (open IssueDetailDrawer for the matching group on mount) and Keywords (scroll-to + highlight row). Cockpit and Engine should share one openWorkQueueItem helper so the two handlers can never drift again.
recommendations.rationale: This deletes the single most expensive step in the highest-frequency journey (J1's unbounded re-find on a 47-fold or 3.9-fold destination). The receiver pattern, param validation, and contract-test scaffolding (tests/contract/tab-deep-link-wiring.test.ts) already exist; Engine proves the sender side already knows the richer params.
recommendations.tradeoffs: A ?focus= receiver on Site Audit needs a defined miss behavior (item resolved since the queue snapshot) — reuse the existing invalid-param InlineBanner pattern rather than silently landing at root.

recommendations.recommendation: Render ENGINE_LENSES as a visible jump control in the Engine's Toolbar — a Segmented or LensSwitcher (both DS primitives, forms/) bound to state.setLens, sitting beside the 'Insights Engine · workspace' header. 'Moves' becomes one click from landing; 'Operations' stops being a page-bottom secret.
recommendations.rationale: The entire receiver already works: ?lens= validates, focuses, and scrollIntoViews the section (EngineSurface.tsx:500-506). Six lenses exist with zero visible UI — this is the audit's 'dead URL vocabulary' made load-bearing instead of deleted, and it collapses J3's 2-fold scroll to one click without moving any section.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Adds one more control row to a surface the ceremony census already flags; the alternative (moving BackingMovesQueue above POV/signals) is a bigger IA change that would need owner sign-off on the read-first spine, so ship the jump nav first.

recommendations.recommendation: Give Requests a real inbox spine: (a) default the tab to whichever section has the newest unanswered item (or add a recency-ordered 'All' union list as the landing view); (b) add a pending-replies badge to the Requests NavItem and generalize RebuiltSidebar's badge wiring from the hard-coded content-pipeline pair (lines 403, 423) into a per-item badge map so collapsed CLIENT-FACING still shows the count on the group header.
recommendations.rationale: J5 is the 'a human is waiting' journey; today the operator lands on Deliverables, guesses among three tabs, and gets zero nav signal that a reply exists. The badge plumbing (pendingContentRequests prop, group-collapse fallback) already exists for one item — generalizing it is mechanical. Admin-side section routing can mirror the client inbox-section-routing contract the platform already documents.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Needs a cheap server count for unanswered replies (distinct from pendingContentRequests); a union landing list means normalizing four legacy panes' items — start with default-tab + badge, defer the union list.

recommendations.recommendation: Fix the J6 inversion: move RecordPublishedWorkCard below the TabBar (or fold it into a 'Record work' secondary button in the surface header that opens a Drawer — the DS overlay primitive), and remove the 'tab-deeplink-ok' waiver by wiring outcome tabs to ?tab= per the platform convention so Cockpit KPIs and ⌘K can link straight to Scorecard/Wins.
recommendations.rationale: The readback is the daily job ('did our work land?'); recording is occasional. The dataset and audit both confirmed the inversion, and deep-linkable tabs restore consistency with the rebuild's flagship URL-state gain (audit §7.1). External ingestion (PR #1470) made the form matter, but a header action preserves it at equal discoverability without taxing every readback visit.
recommendations.tradeoffs: The form-first order was presumably chosen to drive ingestion adoption; a drawer keeps it one click away but no longer ambient — acceptable since the Cockpit send-stream already nudges recording.

recommendations.recommendation: Close the Site Audit per-page repair gap inside the existing drawer: add per-instance action rows (Accept / Send for that page) or an explicit 'Apply to all N affected pages' with a preview list and per-page results, replacing the Hide-only instance rows. Pair it with converting the issue list to the Page Intelligence master/detail idiom (list pane with internal scroll + detail pane) to retire the 47-fold canyon.
recommendations.rationale: J2 is otherwise the best-designed journey (repair + notify + note in one drawer, honest scope label post-sweep) — but a 14-page group at 14 drawer round-trips is the audit's top validated capability loss (§5 #14). Both fixes reuse shipped idioms: the drawer already lists instances; Page Intelligence already proves the master/detail container at 1.0 fold.
recommendations.tradeoffs: 'Apply to all N' is a bulk write to the live site — it must inherit the Accept-all confirm-with-preview pattern (ConfirmDialog, per-page progress) rather than a bare loop; master/detail is a layout refactor on the audit's largest surface, so stage it behind the same drawer-first sequencing.

recommendations.recommendation: ⌘K truth-in-labels + top-10 action verbs. First relabel the 5 navigate-only 'actions' to 'Open …' (palette rows get the same honesty the sweep gave Cockpit rows). Then ship these 10 verbs: (1) Run Site Audit — keep (startJob('seo-audit')); (2) Scan All Workspaces for Anomalies — keep (anomalies.scan()); (3) Review & send staged moves → seo-strategy?lens=moves (deliberately a jump-to-site, never a blind client-facing send); (4) Fix missing titles/metas → seo-editor with the missing-fields filter preset in the URL; (5) Reply to client requests → requests?tab=requests; (6) Record published work → outcomes (form/drawer target from rec 4); (7) Publish site to Webflow → shared publishSite mutation + ConfirmDialog (real dispatch, like SEO Editor's topbar); (8) Refresh strategy → the Engine generation job via useBackgroundTasks (same start/poll/toast contract as run-audit); (9) Re-run PageSpeed → the performance bulk job path (now guarded post-sweep against the fabricated-zero cache); (10) New content brief → content-pipeline?tab=briefs. Convention: verbs that mutate dispatch through useBackgroundTasks/useToast with the run-audit fallback pattern; verbs that need review context deep-link to the exact action site (lens/tab/focus), never a surface root. Also filter debug workspaces (cascade-debug-*, dbg*) out of the palette and WorkspaceSelector behind a workspace flag or name convention.
recommendations.rationale: The palette already owns the correct dispatch skeleton (startJob + toast + honest fallback in the run-audit item, CommandPalette.tsx:170-194) — extending it is pattern application. Client-facing sends stay two-keystroke-safe by landing at the staging/review site instead of firing, which respects the trust-spine posture the sweep just paid for. Debug-workspace leakage is dataset pattern 4's second half.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Real-dispatch verbs need per-verb availability checks (linked site, staged count) surfaced as honest sub-text like run-audit's 'Link a site to run audits'; a stale palette that fires a job the surface would have blocked is worse than navigation-only.

recommendations.recommendation: Unify the SEO Editor bulk send: one 'Send N to client' button on the selection bar that submits both target types (showing a 'N static · M CMS' breakdown line), or at minimum co-locate the two buttons under a single labeled group with the mixed-selection count made explicit.
recommendations.rationale: J4's only click waste: a mixed selection today is two sends producing two approval batches for one logical review event. The client-side unified deliverable spine and the 'one Send to client button + optional note' admin convention (docs/workflows/ui-vocabulary.md) both point the same direction.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Static and CMS approvals ride different server spines; a merged button either needs a combined batch endpoint or fires two mutations behind one control — the latter must report partial failure truthfully (per generation-runtime-integrity), not a single success toast.

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt one name per destination across sidebar, registry, and palette: move the RebuiltSidebar GROUP_PRESENTATION label overrides ('Insights Engine', 'Keywords', 'Asset Manager', 'Content Pipeline') into navRegistry flagBehavior.labelWhenOn entries (the mechanism 'home'→'Cockpit' already uses), and replace CommandPalette's legacy PALETTE_GROUP_LABELS zone headers with the rebuilt group presentation. Delete the sidebar overrides in the same commit.
recommendations.rationale: The triple taxonomy (audit B6's surviving half) taxes every journey at its first step and breaks palette fuzzy-search on the name the operator just read in the sidebar. The registry is already the declared single source ('nav-registry-ok' comment admits the bypass); flagBehavior is the sanctioned override channel and propagates to breadcrumbs and document.title for free.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Flag-ON relabels ripple into breadcrumb/title tests and any copy that says 'Strategy' or 'Keyword Hub'; do the greps (route-removal-checklist discipline) in the same PR.
